commands.review_summary:
The PR updates four `infer inspect` capability metadata flag lists and exports the metadata for a new invariant test that compares every capability id with registered Commander options.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +31. Total +31 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and v2026.6.9 return `CAPABILITY_METADATA` for inspect while the registered commands include the omitted long options.

target.title: fix(cli): sync infer capability inspect metadata flags with registered options
target.body:
## Summary

`infer inspect --name <id> --json` reports each capability's supported flags from a hand-maintained `CAPABILITY_METADATA` array in `src/cli/capability-cli.ts`. Four entries' `flags` arrays had drifted out of sync with the commands' actually-registered options, so `inspect` reported working flags as **unsupported**:

| capability | registered but missing from inspect metadata |
|---|---|
| `model.run` | `--thinking` (registered at `capability-cli.ts:2147`) |
| `model.auth.login` | `--method` (`:2221`) |
| `model.auth.logout` | `--agent` (`:2239`) |
| `audio.transcribe` | `--language` (`:2427`), `--prompt` (`:2428`) |

The flags work end-to-end (the commands register and honor them) — only the inspect inventory under-reported them, so a user or tool inspecting capabilities wouldn't discover them. The existing tests assert exact `flags` arrays for `image.edit`/`image.generate` but these four ids were unguarded.

This syncs the four arrays to the registered options (in registration order) and adds a **global invariant test** that asserts, for *every* capability id, that its metadata `flags` equal its command's registered long options — closing the drift for good.

### Changes
- `src/cli/capability-cli.ts` — add the missing flags to the 4 `CAPABILITY_METADATA` entries; `export` the array so the test can import it.
- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` — new test "keeps capability inspect metadata flags in sync with each command's registered options": registers the CLI and deep-equals each entry's `flags` against the registered long options walked via `id.split(".")`.

Verified completeness: of all 31 capability ids, only these 4 drifted (every other id — `tts.*`, `video.*`, `web.*`, `embedding.*`, `image.*`, all `.providers` — already matched).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `infer inspect` under-reported the supported flags for 4 capabilities (`--thinking`/`--method`/`--agent`/`--language`/`--prompt`), so working options appeared unsupported in the inspect contract. The metadata now lists exactly what each command registers.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no model / network / device. A `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` harness imports the real `CAPABILITY_METADATA` and `registerCapabilityCli`, registers the CLI, and for each of the 4 ids compares the inspect metadata flags to the command's registered long options. BEFORE is `origin/main`'s metadata (export added in for the harness only).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# harness: real CAPABILITY_METADATA vs registerCapabilityCli registered options
./node_modules/.bin/tsx cap-proof.mts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
BEFORE (origin/main):
  model.run: MATCH=false (inspect omits ["--thinking"])
  audio.transcribe: MATCH=false (inspect omits ["--language","--prompt"])
  model.auth.login: MATCH=false (inspect omits ["--method"])
  model.auth.logout: MATCH=false (inspect omits ["--agent"])
AFTER (this branch):
  model.run: MATCH=true
  audio.transcribe: MATCH=true
  model.auth.login: MATCH=true
  model.auth.logout: MATCH=true
```

Observed result after fix: each of the 4 capabilities' inspect metadata now equals its command's registered options; on `origin/main` each omitted the flags shown above. Clean-slug commands and all other ids were already in sync and are unchanged.

What was not tested: a full `openclaw infer inspect` run against a built CLI (the harness drives the real `registerCapabilityCli` command tree + `CAPABILITY_METADATA` directly, which is what `inspect` reads). The new global invariant test in `capability-cli.test.ts` fails before this change (the drift) and passes after, guarding the contract for every capability going forward.

## Additional checks
- `pnpm test src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` — 98 pass (incl. the new invariant test). Verified fail-before by reverting the 4 metadata arrays → the invariant test fails with the exact drift.
- Formatter (`oxfmt`), static analysis (`oxlint`), and `tsgo:core` pass for the changed files.
- Completeness: the invariant test iterates all 31 capability ids, so its green run is positive proof no other id drifts.
target.author: ly-wang19
